top_signals.title: The Iran global inflation crisis that stubbornly refuses to happen

top_signals.title: UAE's exit from OPEC+ reduced the group's share of crude oil production and capacity
top_signals.summary: On April 28, 2026, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced that it was leaving OPEC, effective on May 1. OPEC was formed in 1960 by Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela, with the stated objective to "coordinate and unify petroleum policies among Member Countries." OPEC is best known for its effect on global crude oil prices.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Jupiter Fund Cut Treasuries to Zero on Rising US Inflation Risks
top_signals.summary: Jupiter Asset Management has cut US Treasury holdings to zero in one of its main bond funds, buying European government notes and adding to its hefty emerging-markets position instead.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Serbia Holds Rates as Election Welfare Plans Signal Price Risks
top_signals.summary: Serbia kept borrowing costs unchanged for a record 22nd month as a government plan for cash handouts before this year’s general election may add to inflation pressures.

top_signals.title: Corporate report: The Payment Systems Regulator Annual Report and Accounts 2025 to 2026
top_signals.summary: This report summarises the Payment Systems Regulator Limited's (PSR) activities over the year 2025 to 2026. The PSR is required to publish this report under Paragraph 7 of Schedule 4 of the Financial Services (Banking Reform) Act 2013.

top_signals.title: Pentagon to explore cheaper replacements for the MQ-9 Reaper
